what_it_does: TypeScript declaration for the DiagramSolver React component: declares DiagramSolverProps (a CVGraph spec plus an optional axis with lod/surface/density) and exports the DiagramSolver function returning JSX.Element, documenting it as the graph solver that computes node positions from relationships per diagram type.
observations: Imports CVGraph from ./cv-nodes — types against the shared node system.
axis is typed loosely here: surface is a plain string (not the CVNode union), lod and density reuse the literal unions.
Doc comment lists the supported types: network·hub·morph·pipeline·timeline·quadrant·tree·compare·stack.
Calls out type:'morph' with state before|after as the animatable network→hub transform that tweens via motion tokens.
Default export plus named export of DiagramSolver.
standouts: The list of diagram types in the comment omits 'stepper' and the capital-raise subtypes (orbital/stacked/spectrum/manifold/fidelity) that the .jsx actually implements — the declaration's documented surface is narrower than the implementation.
conventions: Mirrors the system's pattern of a paired .d.ts + .jsx for each core component.
References the shared CVGraph type rather than redefining a graph shape (single source).
JSDoc on the export describes behaviour, consistent with the heavily-documented .d.ts style.
concerns: axis.surface is typed as string while the sibling cv-nodes.d.ts gives surface a strict union — a looser contract here.
Documented type list is incomplete relative to the implementation (no stepper/orbital/stacked/spectrum/manifold/fidelity), so the declaration under-describes the component.
notes: The typed front door to the graph solver — accurate on props but its prose type-list trails what the .jsx now handles.
questions: Why is axis.surface a bare string here when cv-nodes.d.ts defines a surface union — intentional looseness or drift?
Should the declared type list be kept in lockstep with the new subtype views in the .jsx?
purpose_vs_actual: Mostly matches: it declares the graph solver's props and export as stated, but the descriptive type list lags the implementation's actual supported types.
apparent_themes: typed contract for a core engine component
intention_signals: Trying to give consumers a stable typed handle on the graph solver — its one graph prop and the axis dials — while documenting what relational layouts it can produce.
contribution: Contributes the public type signature of the graph solver (DiagramSolverProps over a CVGraph) that callers and the bridge compile against.
summary_for_embedding: TypeScript declaration of the DiagramSolver React component, taking a CVGraph spec and axis dials and rendering a relationally-laid-out diagram.
intention_for_embedding: To expose a typed contract for the graph solver that computes node positions from relationships per diagram type.
